title: Facebook BGP Outage
storyIntro: You are the lead network engineer. At 15:40 UTC, all internal and external DNS routes for your company's domain vanish from the global routing table. WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook are completely down. Employees are locked out of offices because physical badge readers rely on the network. You must restore BGP routes before billions of users are permanently impacted.
brief: A core router configuration command has severed primary DNS peering routes. Global autonomous system paths are unmapped, locking teams out of physical datacenters.

agents.name: Sarah
agents.message: We are completely off the internet. I need a status on OOB access immediately.

agents.name: Dave
agents.message: I can't even get into the building to manually reset the routers!

timelineEvents: Global DNS resolution drops to zero
winCondition: BGP routes successfully readvertised and propagated.
evolving_logs: [CRITICAL] BGP withdrawal script detected in automation logs
[WARNING] Backup DNS servers offline due to cascade failure
[CRITICAL] Stock price drops 15% as outage continues

incorrect_action_consequences.bgp flush: All remaining BGP routes purged, extending outage globally.
incorrect_action_consequences.force_reboot --all: Critical configuration lost during improper shutdown sequence.

Manager.behavior: coordinates team movements and resource allocation

scenario_specific_outputs.dig facebook.com: ;; connection timed out; no servers could be reached
scenario_specific_outputs.ping facebook.com: ping: cannot resolve facebook.com: Unknown host
